agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive and consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a highlights summary. This report is a yearly overview of the city's finances, services, and major projects from the past year. It is not a new law or fee change — it is a presentation for information and discussion. No decision on new rules or spending is being made at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 proposed, effective July 1
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replacing it with the Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The new bylaw consolidates all development applications (including new development permit types and sign permits), expands delegated authorities, and formalizes fee and refund practices. Supporting amendments to the Building Bylaw and Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw also take effect July 1, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on May 12, 2026 — if approved, prepare for new application procedures and fee structures starting July 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Council vote on proposed Development Procedures Bylaw 2026
New Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 takes effect

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby seeks endorsement for DAP program award application
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards for the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. This is a proposed step to recognize improvements in how the city handles development applications. No new rules or fees are being proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for mobile devices and plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, service plans, and data services for city staff. If approved, this contract would set the terms and costs for the city's mobile phone and data needs for a set period. This is a routine procurement decision and does not directly change any rules or fees for local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on aeration and overseeding contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. If approved, this would mean the city hires a company to aerate (poke small holes in the soil to let air, water, and nutrients reach the roots) and overseed (spread new grass seed over existing lawns) on city-owned land. This is a routine maintenance contract and does not create new rules or fees for businesses. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. road upgrades
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for road upgrades on Victory Street, between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Avenue. If approved, this would fund construction work to improve the road. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been made.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on sidewalk contract for five Burnaby streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for new sidewalks on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. If approved, the project would add or replace sidewalks in these areas. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby's Sidewalk Expansion Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for the Sidewalk Expansion Program (project EB-8022). If approved, this change would alter the terms of the contract for building new sidewalks in the city. The decision is not final — Council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments for Maple Ridge and Surrey
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the Metro 2050 regional growth strategy. One change, proposed by the City of Maple Ridge, would allow development on land near North 256 Street. The other, proposed by the City of Surrey, would allow development in the Hazelmere area. These are site-specific amendments that could affect future growth and land use in those communities. Council has not yet decided what to say in the letters — they will discuss and vote on the content at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to review street width standards for new developments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council has directed city staff to review the current street width standards. This means the city will look at how wide streets need to be for new roads and developments. The review could lead to changes in the rules for future construction projects. No new rules have been passed yet — this is just the first step. Business owners in construction, development, and property management should watch for upcoming public consultations or council votes on any proposed changes.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for public consultation opportunities — this review could affect future development costs and street design requirements.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on 2026 consultant budget cuts list
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal that would direct staff to prepare a detailed list of consultant budget cuts for 2026. If approved, staff would identify which consultant contracts to cut, which departments are affected, and which consultants would keep their funding. This is a first step — no cuts have been made yet, and the final list would come back to Council for a later vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the next council meeting — the list of proposed cuts will be made public before a final vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider an update to the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This proposed plan would set new rules for how trails on Burnaby Mountain are used, maintained, and managed. The plan is not yet approved — Council will review it and give direction at an upcoming meeting. If adopted, it could affect how businesses and the public access and use the trails.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this plan — it could affect trail access for your business or events.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's Bingham Cup 2028 bid
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from the Vancouver Roques Rugby club to support their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is a major international rugby tournament for gay and inclusive teams. Council's support would mean the City backs the bid, which could bring visitors and economic activity to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. This means the city is considering building affordable rental homes on these two properties. The project has not been approved yet — council will discuss and decide on the plan at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review status update on major Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a status update on significant civic projects in April 2026. This is a report to keep Council informed about how major city-building projects are progressing. No decisions or votes are expected at this meeting — it is an information item only. Business owners should watch for future council decisions that may follow from this update, such as project approvals, budget changes, or construction timelines that could affect local traffic, permits, or development opportunities.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on major engineering projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a status update on major engineering projects covering December 2025 to April 2026. This is an information report — no decision or bylaw is being voted on at this time. The update will outline progress on key infrastructure work, but no new rules or requirements for businesses are proposed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - DECEMBER 2025 - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on IT initiatives for early 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the city's IT initiatives for January to March 2026. This is a report to keep council informed about ongoing technology projects and their progress. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time. The update is for information only and does not require a vote on new bylaws or policies.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES - JANUARY TO MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the IT initiatives for January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to review IDEAs in Public Recreation research
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Inclusion, Diversity, Equity and Accessibility (IDEAs) in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College. This proposed project aims to improve access and participation in public recreation for Burnaby's diverse communities. The update outlines the project's purpose, partnership model, progress to date, and emerging insights. No final decision has been made yet — council is set to review the findings and discuss next steps.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
PRCC - INCLUSION, DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND ACCESSIBILITY (IDEAS) IN PUBLIC RECREATION RESEARCH IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College, outlining its purpose, partnership model, and progress to date, while sharing emerging insights and demonstrating how the work supports improved access, inclusion, and participation in public recreation for Burnaby’s diverse communities.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on Burnaby Community Safety Plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review a progress report on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan. This is a proposed update to the plan, which aims to improve safety and well-being across the city. Council will consider the report and decide on next steps for the 2026 update. The plan may affect local businesses if it leads to new safety requirements or community programs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
PSC - SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on Burnaby Fire Department operations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the Burnaby Fire Department's activities up to the end of February 2026. This is a routine report that provides an overview of the department's operations, including any notable incidents, response times, or resource needs. The report is for information only — no bylaw changes or new rules are proposed at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7
PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on RCMP operations and annual performance plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a status update on RCMP policing operations for December 2025 to January 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for October to December 2025. This is an information item only — no vote or bylaw is proposed at this time. The report gives Council a look at recent police activities and how well the RCMP is meeting its yearly goals. Business owners should be aware that this is a routine update, not a new rule or fee change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8
PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed new zoning bylaw that would replace the current Burnaby Zoning Bylaw from 1965. The proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 would update all zoning rules, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines (rules for getting permits to build or change a property) and reassign many properties to new zoning districts. This is a major rewrite of the city's land-use rules that could affect what you can build, how you can use your property, and what permits you need. The decision has not been made yet — this is the first step in the approval process.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to tell Council how the new rules would affect your business or property.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update zoning enforcement rules for 2026 bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. The change would replace the old zoning rules from 1965 with the new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This means that if the new bylaw passes, the city will use the updated 2026 zoning rules when enforcing violations — for example, if a business is using a property in a way that doesn't match its zoning. The proposal is at its first reading stage, so it has not been approved yet. Business owners should watch for the next council meeting and any public hearing dates.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any public hearing dates — this change could affect how zoning rules are enforced against your property.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend affordable housing fee waiver rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the current Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw. Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges are fees the city charges developers to help pay for community needs like parks, daycare, and roads. If approved, this change could affect which housing projects qualify for fee waivers or reductions. The first reading of the amendment is on the agenda for the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect eligibility for fee waivers on future housing projects.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create new protections for tenants whose homes are being redeveloped. The Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 aims to give renters more rights and support when their landlord plans to demolish or renovate their building. This is a first reading, meaning it is an early step in the approval process — the bylaw has not been passed yet and could still change before a final vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to tell council your views on the proposed tenant protections.
agenda_briefs.industries: Tenant, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protection enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces rules that protect tenants. If approved, it could mean new or stronger penalties for landlords who break tenant protection rules. The amendment is still a proposal — it has not been passed yet. Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how tenant complaints are enforced against landlords.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby mid-year fee changes up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed mid-year changes to the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, this amendment would update various fees the city charges for permits, licences, and services. The exact fees being changed are not listed in this agenda item, but the bylaw covers everything from business licences to building permits. Business owners should watch for the council decision to see if fees affecting their operations will increase or change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this bylaw amendment — it could affect your business licence or permit fees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update rules for the city's e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program. If approved, it would change how these vehicles are regulated on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to adopt the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if adopted, new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations or deliveries.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike pilot program in parks up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The proposed amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw would create temporary rules for where and how these devices can be used. If approved, the pilot would let riders use e-bikes and e-scooters on designated park pathways, but not on trails or in natural areas. The pilot is meant to test how these vehicles affect park safety and enjoyment before any permanent rules are made. This is a proposed change only — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the pilot rules will affect where you can ride e-bikes and e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw changes for e-bike and e-scooter pilot
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This proposed change is part of a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, it would update how the city enforces rules for these devices — for example, how tickets or fines are issued. The decision has not been made yet; council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rules are enforced in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets new property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings (called 'improvements') in the city for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set property tax rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property owners pay in municipal taxes next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on swapping office for 292 more homes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would let a developer scrap planned office space and add 292 more market strata residential units instead. The project is at Grand Promenade, Austin Road, and Gatineau Place. Originally approved in 2024 for two towers (80 and 73 storeys) with a 13-storey retail and office podium, the new plan removes the office use entirely. This is a rezoning amendment — a change to the official zoning rules for that land — and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
